### Post by Tenkunowind on 2009-12-10
Hi, i'm having quite a hard time getting my S-video TV to work with a higher resolution, it is set to 800x600, now i will show you  my xorg.conf

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "es"
    Option        "XkbVariant"    "nodeadkeys"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
        Option "Monitor-S-video" "TV"
        Option "TVStandard" "ntsc"
        Option "ForceTVOut" "True"
        Option "TVDACLoadDetect" "True" 
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "VGA-0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "TV"
        Modeline  "1024x768_75.00"  81.80  1024 1080 1192 1360  768 769 772 802  -HSync +Vsync
        Option "PreferredMode" "1024x768_75.00" 
        Option "RightOf" "VGA-0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                    Depth   24
                    Modes   "1024x768"
                    Virtual 2048 768
        EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
    Screen        "Default Screen"
EndSection


```The modeline was get from writing gtf 1024 768 75  on the  terminal, i tried with 
```
xrandr --newmode  "1024x768_75.00"  81.80  1024 1080 1192 1360  768 769 772 802  -HSync +Vsync
```I get no message at all, and with xrandr --addmode,  i always get:

```
user@user-desktop:~$ xrandr --addmode S-video 1024x768
X Error of failed request:  BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes)
  Major opcode of failed request:  156 (RANDR)
  Minor opcode of failed request:  18 ()
  Serial number of failed request:  16
  Current serial number in output stream:  17

```I had been looking around the net, but i guess i am doing something wrong

---

### Post by Marran77 on 2010-03-28
xrandr can't output more than 800x600 in S-video. Change 1024x786 to 800x600 and you're ready to go

---
